evals.prompt: Create a migration for a new 'team_invitations' table with email, role, status, and expiry fields
evals.expected_output: Creates timestamped migration file with UUID primary key, company_id FK, RLS policy using profiles table join, indexes on company_id and foreign keys

assertions.name: uuid-primary-key
assertions.description: Table has id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid()

assertions.name: company-id-fk
assertions.description: Table has company_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES companies(id) ON DELETE CASCADE

assertions.name: rls-enabled
assertions.description: Includes ALTER TABLE team_invitations ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY

assertions.name: rls-policy-pattern
assertions.description: RLS policy checks company_id via profiles table join (SELECT company_id FROM profiles WHERE id = auth.uid())

assertions.name: if-not-exists
assertions.description: Uses CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS for idempotency

assertions.name: reads-existing-migrations
assertions.description: Reads latest migration in supabase/migrations/ to understand current schema

evals.prompt: Add a 'last_synced_at' timestamp column to the social_accounts table
evals.expected_output: Creates ALTER TABLE migration with ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS, no need for RLS changes since table already has it

assertions.name: alter-not-create
assertions.description: Uses ALTER TABLE, not CREATE TABLE, since the table already exists

assertions.name: if-not-exists-column
assertions.description: Uses ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS for idempotency

assertions.name: verify-step
assertions.description: Runs npx tsc --noEmit after applying migration to check types
